reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (headless Chrome, real download+run, light+dark, mobile+desktop) — revision 1

rubric.evidence: REAL WASM run in headless Chrome (fresh profile → Download → ready): onnx-community/Bio_ClinicalBERT-ONNX, transformers.js 3.7.5, q8, AutoModelForMaskedLM. 'The patient was admitted with acute [MASK] failure.' → respiratory 95.4% (logit 17.02), heart 3.6% (13.73), liver 0.6%, kidney 0.1%, lung 0.1%. 0 console errors, 0 network failures. screenshots: /home/paulkinlan/tmp/w14dshots/bio-clinicalbert-desktop-dark.png, -mobile-light.png
rubric.notes: Runnability + domain quality verified BEFORE build; BioBERT-v1.1 rejected (broken flat head at q8 AND fp32); SciBERT scivocab ONNX rejected (feature-extraction, no MLM head) — recorded as blocked scibert-fill-mask.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — logits tensor [1, seq, 28996]; per-mask top-k with real logit + softmax probability; multi-mask supported. Domain-correct output on clinical text (respiratory/heart/liver/kidney for 'acute [MASK] failure'; 'conversion' 81% for 'catalyzes the [MASK] of glucose'; 'imaging' 92% for 'magnetic resonance [MASK]' verified pre-build). Correct shape + domain semantics, not fake.

At-a-glance declares the exact model id (onnx-community/Bio_ClinicalBERT-ONNX, linked to card + original emilyalsentzer repo), task (fill-mask), backend (WebAssembly), dtype (q8), ~131 MB, MIT, vocab 28,996 cased. Inference off the main thread (worker.js). conformance declares-* auto-checks expected pass.

Agent verdict — fresh profile showed 'Download model (~131 MB)'; after load the shared createModelLoader auto-inits to 'ready' (download-required → downloading → ready observed) with a 'Clear cached model' control, then auto-runs the default fill. Returning visit auto-inits from cache with no Load click. The general bert-base-cased comparator loads only on demand (Compare), never silently.

Agent verdict — streamed download progress; Retry on failure; #runStatus live region (role=status aria-live) reports 'add a [MASK]' / errors; a separate #cmpStatus announces general-BERT download progress on Compare; offline reuse via the transformers.js cache. Runs on the WASM path (no GPU). conformance live-status-region/errors-on-the-page expected pass.

Agent verdict — sentence input, 6 clinical example chips, Insert-[MASK] button, top-k slider, and clickable prediction rows (fill the mask) verified operable end-to-end (real fill run). The 'Compare vs general BERT' button is present, keyboard-focusable and enables on ready; its two-model end-to-end run is component-verified (both Bio_ClinicalBERT AND the general bert-base-cased comparator were each independently confirmed to return correct output — clinical respiratory 95.4%; general bert-base-cased respiratory 30.2%/heart 28.6%/kidney + 'conversion' 85.4% + 'imaging' 29.3% — and compare() is a thin wrapper calling each model's verified runOne); a single end-to-end compare screenshot was not captured because the 263 MB two-model download exceeded the sandbox timeout (a download-speed limit, not a code fault). Ladder pages (basics/practical scoreCandidates/wild arena/multi-model MiniLM) each load with 8–9 controls, 0 console errors, 0 overflow (headless matrix).
rubric.notes: Agent-verified on both device classes; compare path component-verified (see evidence).

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — predictions, logits, candidate scores and the clinical-vs-general comparison are all produced by on-device AutoModelForMaskedLM runs (real clinical output recorded above; the general comparator's output independently verified). No canned result anywhere — the compare simply renders two real model runs side by side.

Agent verdict — no content images (text model); every button has an accessible name; the sentence <input> has an associated <label>; live regions announce state; prediction rows are keyboard-activatable buttons. conformance buttons-have-names/images-have-alt expected pass (vacuous for images).

Agent verdict — desktop 1280 (light+dark) and mobile 360×740 DPR3 render with no horizontal overflow (measured 0 px on index + all 4 ladder pages, both classes); the clinical-vs-general comparison grid collapses to one column under 560px; prediction grid hides the logit column on narrow screens (shared FILLMASK_CSS). screenshots reviewed (w14dshots/bio-clinicalbert-*).

Agent verdict — the masked-LM forward pass + softmax over 28,996 vocab runs in a Web Worker; typing is debounced (350 ms); the compare runs both models in the worker sequentially. Main thread stays responsive; 0 console errors during interaction. guidance identify-inp-causes + break-up-long-tasks applied.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep INP low — keep heavy JS off the interaction path.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the masked-LM forward pass + softmax runs in a Web Worker; typing is debounced (350 ms) so it never triggers a blocking run on each keystroke.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js runs AutoModelForMaskedLM; clinicalfillmask.js reuses the debounce; 95.4% respiratory result in-worker, page responsive, 0 console errors

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Offload / break up long main-thread tasks.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inference (including the two-model compare) is off the main thread; the UI only renders returned top-k / candidate scores.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js handles fill/compare/scoreCandidates; index.html only renders

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid layout; wrap chips; collapse multi-column grids on narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — flex-wrap example chips; the clinical-vs-general .cmp-grid collapses to 1 column under 560px; the prediction grid hides its logit column on narrow screens.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: overflow=0 at 360px on index + all ladder pages; mobile screenshot reviewed

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Accessible form controls with clear, associated labels.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the sentence <input> and candidate <input> have associated <label>s; the top-k slider is labelled; example chips are real buttons.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: run panel markup; conformance buttons-have-names derived pass

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label controls; announce state in a live region.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live #runStatus + #cmpStatus, labelled controls, prediction rows are keyboard-activatable buttons that fill the mask.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page nav.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in the shared public/styles.css.

openQuestions: Bio_ClinicalBERT is cased and uses the bert-base-cased vocab (28,996) — the comparison against bert-base-cased is a fair same-vocab domain test, but the true SciBERT scivocab (31,090) MLM story cannot be shown because no SciBERT scivocab ONNX ships a masked-LM head (recorded as blocked scibert-fill-mask).

followUpGoals.goal: Add a domain-specific downstream demo (clinical NER or clinical sentence retrieval) that reuses the Bio_ClinicalBERT encoder, showing the same weights power more than fill-mask.
Re-check upstream for a SciBERT scivocab masked-LM ONNX export; if one appears, build scibert-fill-mask (currently blocked with evidence) to show the scivocab-tokenization angle.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for bio-clinicalbert (Bio_ClinicalBERT, a clinical/biomedical masked LM trained on MIMIC-III). Chosen as the runnable, verified domain fill-mask after SciBERT scivocab ONNX (feature-extraction, no MLM head) and BioBERT-v1.1 (broken flat head at q8+fp32) were both rejected and the literal scibert-fill-mask slug recorded blocked with evidence. Real download + on-device run verified in headless Chrome ('acute [MASK] failure' → respiratory 95.4%, logit 17.02), light+dark + mobile+desktop screenshots reviewed (0 overflow, comparison grid collapses on mobile), console + network clean. Signature see-inside: a same-vocabulary clinical-vs-general (bert-base-cased) head-to-head where the domain model concentrates mass on the medically-correct word — the domain gap made measurable. Distinct from the general BERT/ALBERT/XLM-R/ModernBERT fill-mask pages.
